name: Bengali
register: Contemporary reverent Bengali Bible register (cholito-bhasha), matching the Bible Society of India Bengali Bible / Common-Language (Mangalsamachar) tradition that Bengali Christians read and pray from, not the older sadhu-bhasha Carey diction. Third-person deity takes honorific verb agreement and তাঁর; the Father is addressed with the intimate-yet-reverent তুমি of the Bengali Lord's Prayer (হে আমাদের স্বর্গস্থ পিতা), the register of a beloved child speaking to their own father.
quote_convention: Straight double quotes " " around speech, matching the English source verbatim in count and placement; single quotes ' ' for a quotation nested inside a quotation.
poetry_convention: Preserve the English poetry line breaks exactly: one Bengali line per English line, same count, order, and indent (the numeric indent is copied, only the line text is translated). Hebrew parallelism is kept across the line pair; do not merge or split lines unless a native reviewer rebreaks them.

glossary.gloss: our Father who is in heaven
glossary.note: Divine name. Matches the vocative of the Bengali Lord's Prayer (Matt 6:9), the phrase Bengali Christians know best; স্বর্গস্থ = 'in heaven'.
Primary filial divine name. পিতা is the reverent word for 'father' used of God in the Bengali Bible. NEVER revert to ঈশ্বর / সদাপ্রভু.
Filial divine name (definite). Bengali has no article; definiteness carries through honorific verb agreement and context. Bare পিতা, capital-F, is preferred over demonstrative সেই পিতা.
Filial divine name. স্বর্গীয় = adjectival 'heavenly'; kin to the fuller স্বর্গস্থ পিতা of 'our Heavenly Father'.

Aramaic address retained + Bengali gloss (Mark 14:36; Rom 8:15; Gal 4:6). আব্বা doubles as the everyday Bengali word for 'dad', so the intimacy Paul intends lands natively — then পিתা reverently names it.
REVERSION TOKEN. Standard Bengali Christian word for God (not the Muslim-idiom আল্লাহ/খোদা). Use ONLY where the English keeps 'God' as God-as-God; never as a substitute for 'our Father'.

REVERSION TOKEN. The standard Bengali OT rendering of the covenant name. Use ONLY where the English keeps 'the LORD'; never as a substitute for 'our Father'. (Generic title 'Lord' for Jesus = প্রভু, distinct.)
Standard Bengali Bible term (John 3).
Relational (of the Father), not by-identity; keeps the filial stem পিতা in the genitive পিতার.
The classic Bengali Bible compound for the title (John 3), literally 'man-son'.
The Son (Jesus). Bare পুত্র, definite by context/honorific agreement.
Christological title where the English retains 'God' → ঈশ্বর. Native reviewer to confirm vs the filial পিতার পুত্র ('Son of the Father').
John 3:16. একমাত্র = 'sole/only'; reviewer may prefer the classic একজাত ('only-begotten') of the older Bengali text.
FHB renders 'kingdom of God' filially (John 3:3,5): the standard ঈশ্বরের রাজ্য is refiled onto পিতার.
Standard Bengali Bible spelling (also modern যিশু).
Standard Bengali Bible term (John 3:28; also modern খ্রিষ্ট).
Bengali transliteration of the Hebrew title (John 1:41), distinct from the Greek-derived খ্রীষ্ট.
Standard Bengali Bible term — as in পুরাতন নিয়ম / নতুন নিয়ম (Old/New Covenant).
Renders Hebrew hesed (loyal covenant love). অবিচল = 'unwavering'. Reviewer to confirm vs the traditional দয়া / করুণা ('loving-kindness, mercy') used for hesed in some Bengali texts.
Renders Hebrew racham / Greek splanchnizomai (gut-level tender mercy). Standard করুণা; reviewer may prefer মমতা for the visceral, womb-deep tenderness of racham.
Standard Bengali Bible term.
John 3:16. অনন্ত = 'without end'.
John 3:3,7. নতুন জন্ম = 'new birth'; reviewer to confirm against the 'from above' sense (উপর হইতে জন্ম) some Bengali texts carry.
